reference: Hi, I am a thirty-four-year-old female lupus patient. I am five feet six inches tall and weigh one hundred and eighty-eight pounds. Recently, I have experienced unexplained weight loss of sixteen% of my body weight over the past six months. My rheumatologist is investigating with a chest X-ray and laboratory tests. All results are not back yet, but I do know that my liver values are elevated, my sedimentation rate is high, and my white blood cell count is low; however, my thyroid function came back normal. I have not been in a flare for at least nine months. I believe he is looking for signs of lymphoma. Do any of these results point in that direction?
text: I am a 34-year-old female lupus patient. I am 5 feet 6 inches tall and weigh 188 pounds. Recently, I have experienced unexplained weight loss of 16% of my body weight over the past six months. My rheumatologist is investigating with a chest x-ray and laboratory tests. All results are not back yet, but I do know that my liver values are elevated, my sedimentation rate is high, and my white blood cell count is low. However, my thyroid function came back normal. I have not been in a flare for at least nine months. I believe he is looking for signs of lymphoma. Just see if these results point in that direction.
